perturbation.original_text: Term has the meaning set out in Clause 3.1;
perturbation.changed_text: Term has the meaning set out in Clause 3.1; Notwithstanding Clause 3.1, for purposes of Intellectual Property Rights, 'Term' shall be interpreted as the duration of patent protection available for SHBV Technologies.
perturbation.explanation: By adding a contradictory definition of 'Term' specifically for Intellectual Property Rights, it creates ambiguity about the duration of the agreement regarding intellectual property, potentially extending beyond the initially defined ten years from clause 3.1. This contradicts the overall term definition and could lead to disputes over intellectual property usage after the initial agreement period. The pertubation of 'Term' definition, makes the Intellectual Property clause to extend beyond the contract term, in order to maintain patent protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The licence granted in Clause 10.2 above will automatically expire at the end of the Term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a new definition of 'Term' specifically for Intellectual Property Rights, which directly contradicts the original definition set out in Clause 3.1. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict with Clause 10.2, which states that the license granted will automatically expire at the end of the 'Term' of the agreement. If the 'Term' for intellectual property rights is longer than the general 'Term' of the agreement as defined in clause 3.1, the license could be interpreted to extend beyond the intended duration, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: SHBV agrees that it shall manufacture and supply to W2E (or as it shall direct), W2E Equipment, in each case in accordance with the terms of the manufacturing agreement ("Manufacturing Agreement") to be entered into by the Parties on or following the entry into this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SHBV agrees that it shall use reasonable endeavours to manufacture and supply to W2E (or as it shall direct), W2E Equipment, in each case in accordance with the terms of the manufacturing agreement ("Manufacturing Agreement") to be entered into by the Parties on or following the entry into this Agreement. However, SHBV will not be liable for failure to manufacture or supply W2E equipment.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "SHBV agrees that it shall manufacture and supply" to "SHBV agrees that it shall use reasonable endeavours to manufacture and supply", makes the obligation of SHBV to supply equipment subject to its efforts rather than a definite commitment, also adding "SHBV will not be liable for failure to manufacture or supply W2E equipment" relieves SHBV of liability for non-supply. In contradiction to the obligations and roles within the original text, SHBV now will not be liable for the equipment supply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is the intention of the Parties that W2E (or such other entity that it shall nominate) shall be the prime contractor entering into agreements with its customers, with SHBV acting as a subcontractor under the Manufacturing Agreement for the responsibilities that fall to SHBV. The Parties agree that it is the intention that they shall have the following responsibilities to fulfil under their agreements with W2E Customer:
perturbation.justification: The original text states SHBV 'shall manufacture and supply', indicating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'reasonable endeavors' and adds a clause relieving SHBV of liability for failure to supply. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which casts SHBV as a subcontractor with 'responsibilities to fulfill' under the Manufacturing Agreement. By removing liability for non-supply, SHBV's role as a responsible subcontractor is undermined, creating a strong in-text contradiction, ambiguity, and undermining the agreement. This is an instance of an ambiguity leading to in-text contradiction as per the definitions, where the definition of responsibilities and obligation are changed.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that during the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, either on its own account or through its employees or agents or otherwise or on behalf of any other person, firm, company or other organisation and other than by general advertising, solicit, interfere with, procure or entice away (or, in each case, attempt so to do), either directly or indirectly, any employee or contractor of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that during the first year of this Agreement it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, either on its own account or through its employees or agents or otherwise or on behalf of any other person, firm, company or other organisation and other than by general advertising, solicit, interfere with, procure or entice away (or, in each case, attempt so to do), either directly or indirectly, any employee or contractor of the other Party. After the first year, either party may solicit employees from the other party for roles that do not directly compete with existing projects under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the non-solicitation agreement to the first year and allowing solicitation afterward for non-competing roles, this creates ambiguity and contradiction. The first sentence says that the solicitation of employees cannot be done, but in the following sentence, it says it can be done. This contradicts the original intent of preventing employee poaching throughout the term. The lack of clear definition on what 'directly compete' means makes enforcement difficult. Soliciting employee in non competing roles opens to solicit anyone after 1 year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that during the first year of this Agreement it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, either on its own account or through its employees or agents or otherwise or on behalf of any other person, firm, company or other organisation and other than by general advertising, solicit, interfere with, procure or entice away (or, in each case, attempt so to do), either directly or indirectly, any employee or contractor of the other Party. After the first year, either party may solicit employees from the other party for roles that do not directly compete with existing projects under this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction by initially prohibiting solicitation of employees and then, after one year, permitting solicitation for roles not directly competing with existing projects. This directly contradicts the original intent to prevent employee poaching throughout the term, as roles that are not in direct competition may open a loophole to still solicit emplyees. The contradictory section is the paragraph where it initially states there is a 6-month restriction after the agreement, and the modified section opens it after the first year for roles that do not compete with existing projects. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict, making it difficult to enforce the non-solicitation agreement.
